site.title: Software Engineer — Flutter, Dart & Go
site.description: Software engineer building and operating cross-platform products and platform tooling with Flutter, Dart, Go, PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker, and Kubernetes.
site.social_description: Cross-platform product engineering, platform tooling, and verified open-source work.

sources.label: GitHub
sources.scope: Public repositories, merged pull requests, and pull-request status

sources.label: LinkedIn
sources.scope: Current role, professional timeline, and public experience descriptions

profile.headline: I build cross-platform products and the systems that keep them reliable.
profile.summary: My work spans Flutter clients, local and real-time data, native integrations, Go services, queues, and production infrastructure. I stay close to the boundary where product behaviour becomes a systems problem.

experience.summary: Build and maintain production applications across Android and iOS tablets plus Linux, macOS, and Windows desktops. The work includes offline-first SQLite and Drift data, native integrations, localisation, real-time synchronisation, release engineering, and reliability on point-of-sale hardware.
Delivered mobile and web product work spanning Flutter, React and Next.js, REST and WebSocket data flows, vehicle tracking, and Google and Apple Maps integrations.
Shipped Android and iOS publishing applications with maintainable presentation boundaries, API integration, localisation, and store-ready release workflows.
Formal study in algorithms, data structures, databases, software design, and team-based product development.

contributions.title: Make Firebase core loading deterministic on WebKit
contributions.problem: Component bundles could execute before firebase-app.js was available, producing an initialization race in WebKit browsers.
contributions.change: Load the Firebase core script before component bundles and preserve deterministic dependency order.

contributions.title: Reset unknown dropdown initial values on first build
contributions.problem: A dropdown could begin with a value that did not exist in its items and fail before normal field reset behaviour ran.
contributions.change: Normalize the first build and cover the regression with widget tests.

contributions.title: Treat SQLite TRUE and 1 defaults as the same schema
contributions.problem: Schema verification reported equivalent SQLite boolean defaults as a migration difference.
contributions.change: Normalize the verifier comparison and add regression coverage for both representations.

contributions.title: Add a Fiber and Asynq background-jobs recipe
contributions.problem: The recipe collection lacked a production-shaped example for enqueueing and processing Redis-backed work outside request handlers.
contributions.change: Add a runnable producer, worker, queue configuration, and usage documentation.

contributions.title: Wait for web rendering before the first-frame event
contributions.problem: On SkWasm, flutter-first-frame can fire before the compositor presents visible Flutter content, leaving a measurable blank handoff window.
contributions.change: Wait for outstanding scene renders and the next browser frame before dispatching the event; the pull request is ready for review with seven Chrome renderer and compiler suites passing.

systems.name: Flutter Web Portfolio
systems.summary: A Wasm and SkWasm portfolio used to investigate first-frame timing, semantic document structure, locale transitions, and scroll-directed rendering in Flutter Web.
systems.decision: Keep the semantic document and high-frequency painter paths separate while one measured scroll model directs both.

systems.name: Queue Inspector MCP
systems.summary: An MCP server for inspecting and operating Redis-backed Asynq and BullMQ queues without reaching into storage by hand.
systems.decision: Expose queue state, job detail, retries, and dead letters through explicit operations with conservative defaults.

systems.name: Multi-tenant Gateway
systems.summary: A small single-binary gateway that demonstrates tenant resolution, JWT authentication, per-tenant rate limits, and module boundaries.
systems.decision: Keep tenant context explicit from transport through policy rather than hiding it behind global state.

systems.name: Redis Task Queue
systems.summary: A Redis-backed task queue for Dart with retries, dead-letter handling, and weighted queues.
systems.decision: Make failure states inspectable and scheduling behaviour deterministic without adding a runtime framework.

systems.name: Constellation Particles
systems.summary: A dependency-free particle field for Flutter with pointer interaction and spatial-grid neighbour lookup.
systems.decision: Use spatial partitioning to avoid comparing every particle pair as the scene grows.

story.title: The interface begins before the first pixel.
story.body: Product constraints, data boundaries, rendering, semantics, and recovery paths are one system—not separate polish passes.

story.title: Build for the conditions the product will actually meet.
story.body: Unreliable networks, offline data, native integrations, and release failures belong in the design from the beginning.

story.title: When the boundary is wrong, move the boundary.
story.body: Turn product-level failures into minimal reproductions, tests, and upstream patches instead of hiding them behind local workarounds.

story.title: Leave the next engineer a system they can change.
story.body: Explicit state, typed data, narrow interfaces, observable failure modes, and verified release paths keep software useful after launch.
